sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Pebblebrook Hotel Trust (NYSE:PEB) Price Target Lowered to $9.00 at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for Pebblebrook Hotel Trust (NYSE:PEB) from $10.00 to $9.00, maintaining an "underweight" rating and suggesting a nearly 29% downside. Despite this, the REIT recently surpassed quarterly EPS estimates and issued Q1 and FY 2026 guidance. Analyst ratings for PEB are mixed, with a consensus "Reduce" rating and an average price target of $12.33.

feed.title: ONEOK (NYSE:OKE) Price Target Raised to $113.00
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has raised its price target for ONEOK (NYSE:OKE) to $113.00 from $104.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating and suggesting a 25.13% upside. This new target is significantly higher than the MarketBeat consensus target of $89.50, which is based on a split of nine Buy and nine Hold ratings from other analysts. ONEOK recently topped Q4 EPS estimates and provided 2026 guidance, with the stock trading around $90.30.

feed.title: Axon Enterprise (AXON) Stock Drops 8% as Legal Risk and Analyst Cuts Pile Up
feed.summary: Axon Enterprise (AXON) stock plummeted 8.21% to a 52-week low due to mounting legal risks concerning its Scottsdale headquarters project, analyst target reductions, and a broader selloff in the SaaS sector. Despite strong Q4 bookings growth and positive fiscal 2026 revenue guidance, market sentiment has shifted, with the stock flagged as overvalued by InvestingPro. An upcoming court hearing on the headquarters lawsuits is a critical event for investors.

feed.title: Raymond James lowers MSCI stock price target to $700 on market concerns
feed.summary: Raymond James has lowered its price target for MSCI Inc. (NYSE:MSCI) to $700 from $710, while maintaining a Strong Buy rating due to anticipated headwinds from a March equity market downturn. Despite the reduced target, MSCI remains Raymond James' top pick within Information Services, citing strong sales momentum, limited AI risk, solid fundamentals with 9.75% revenue growth, and aggressive share buybacks by management. The firm also highlights MSCI's strategic acquisitions and partnerships aimed at enhancing its financial services offerings.

feed.title: Flying Delta? Your Next Trip Just Got More Expensive
feed.summary: Delta Airlines is increasing its checked-bag fees for domestic and select short-haul international flights due to surging jet fuel prices, which have risen nearly 88 percent following the conflict in the Middle East. The first checked bag will now cost $45, and a second will be $55, though frequent flyers, premium passengers, and co-branded credit card holders may be exempt from these increases. This move reflects the airline industry's trend of passing increased operational costs to consumers.

feed.title: MGM Stock Risk/Reward Points to Upside, Says Analyst
feed.summary: Stifel analyst Steven Wieczynski reiterates a "buy" rating on MGM Resorts International (NYSE: MGM) stock, despite paring his price target to $48 from $50. He believes the stock offers a compelling risk/reward at current levels, anticipating a second-half 2026 rebound for the Las Vegas Strip and noting the strength of MGM's digital, regional casino, and Macau businesses. Wieczynski suggests that the market has overly discounted Strip leisure weakness and that forward indicators are encouraging for investors.

feed.title: Ball Corp. boosts sustainable operations for a tighter world: 84% renewable energy coverage in ’25
feed.summary: Ball Corporation’s 2025 annual and sustainability report highlights how sustainability intertwines with profitability, showing significant advancements in renewable energy coverage and emission reductions. The company's strategic operational efficiencies, supply chain tightening, and focus on regional manufacturing are designed to navigate evolving policy environments like US tariffs and European CBAM. Ball's efforts underscore a shift towards sustainability as a core resilience strategy against geopolitical and market volatilities, rather than just an expense.

feed.title: A Look At PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust (PMT) Valuation As Shares Trade Near Recent Levels
feed.summary: PennyMac Mortgage Investment Trust (PMT) is currently trading at $11.95, which is below the average analyst price target, but its valuation appears high with a P/E of 12.1x, exceeding its industry and peer averages. The company has seen a decline in earnings and narrowing profit margins over the past five years, and a Discounted Cash Flow model suggests the stock is overvalued at $8.75 per share, indicating potential risks if mortgage credit conditions worsen. Investors should consider these mixed signals and further investigate the risks and rewards associated with PMT.

feed.title: CPI Card Group partners with Data Center Inc to expand digital wallet card access
feed.summary: CPI Card Group has announced a partnership with Data Center Inc (DCI) to allow cardholders to add their cards to digital wallets before receiving physical cards. This collaboration aims to provide earlier and more secure access to payment features, aligning with ongoing industry efforts to increase the adoption of digital and contactless payment solutions. The initiative builds on CPI Card Group's previous efforts in push provisioning and promoting metal cards for enhanced cardholder engagement.

feed.title: Plus Therapeutics (PSTV) Stock: CNSide Diagnostic Secures AMA Billing Code Despite Share Decline
feed.summary: Despite a 4.80% decline in its stock price, Plus Therapeutics (PSTV) announced a significant milestone: its CNSide diagnostic system has secured a dedicated AMA billing code (0640U), effective July 1, 2026. This proprietary code will streamline reimbursement processes for the cerebrospinal fluid tumor cell diagnostic, enhancing its commercial infrastructure and market penetration in CNS oncology. The CNSide platform helps identify malignant cells in CSF, addressing a critical need in treating metastatic central nervous system malignancies.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Urges Investors To buy McDonald’s Dip As Stock Falls 10%
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has reiterated an Outperform rating and a $355 price target for McDonald's (MCD) stock, seeing a buying opportunity after a nearly 10% dip from its February high. Key catalysts include the revamped McValue platform and a new McCafe beverage lineup. The investment bank's thesis highlights underappreciated unit growth, strong international sales, and improving margins for the fast-food giant.

feed.title: Arm’s stock is falling, as Morgan Stanley throws cold water on all the Wall Street hype
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Lee Simpson has downgraded Arm Holdings (ARM) to equal weight, suggesting that further stock momentum will be challenging despite a nearly 40% rally since February. The analyst cites ongoing Qualcomm litigation and increased competition as factors that could hinder a sustained rally for Arm shares. This adjustment comes even as Arm expands into selling its own CPUs, aiming to capitalize on the CPU market.

feed.title: Deere & Co agrees to pay $99 million to settle 'right to repair' lawsuit
feed.summary: Deere & Co. has agreed to a $99 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit alleging the company monopolized repair services and withheld repair software. The lawsuit, filed in 2022, accused Deere of forcing farmers to use authorized dealers for repairs, leading to higher costs. Despite denying wrongdoing, Deere settled to resolve the issue and noted it still faces separate litigation from the Federal Trade Commission regarding similar "right to repair" claims.

feed.title: Walmart launches La Roche-Posay in stores
feed.summary: Walmart is expanding its beauty offerings by launching the skin care brand La Roche-Posay in 1,460 stores nationwide. This partnership includes training Walmart pharmacists to provide specialized skin care consultations. The move is part of Walmart's broader strategy to make advanced beauty products more accessible and strengthen its position as a leading skin care destination, competing with other major retailers and online platforms like Amazon and TikTok Shop.

feed.title: Arson suspect arrested after massive fire tears through warehouse in Ontario, California
feed.summary: An arson suspect has been detained after a large fire erupted at a Kimberly-Clark warehouse in Ontario, California. The fire started around 12:30 a.m. and prompted a major response from over 100 firefighters. Officials quickly identified the incident as suspicious, leading to an arrest, though all employees in the warehouse at the time safely evacuated.

feed.title: EQT Corp. stock: Undervalued at 62% discount with strong cash flow potential
feed.summary: EQT Corp., a leading natural gas producer, is trading at a significant discount to its intrinsic value, potentially offering a buying opportunity for investors. The company recently upsized a debt tender offer, signaling confidence in its balance sheet and proactive debt management. With strong operational efficiency, a vast inventory, and exposure to rising LNG export demand, EQT stands to benefit from global energy transitions, although commodity price volatility and regulatory shifts remain key risks.

feed.title: PSE&G Supports Continued Customer and Community Benefits in Energy Efficiency Triennium 2.5
feed.summary: PSE&G is advocating for the extension of its energy efficiency programs through June 2028, known as Triennium 2.5, to continue delivering significant benefits to customers and communities in New Jersey. These programs have already resulted in nearly $900 million in annual energy savings and avoided carbon emissions equivalent to removing over 500,000 cars, while also supporting local jobs and businesses. The company emphasizes the importance of these initiatives in managing energy costs and contributing to a stable and reliable energy system.

feed.title: New Jersey efficiency push has saved nearly $900M a year, PSE&G says
feed.summary: PSE&G is proposing to extend its New Jersey energy efficiency programs through June 2028, aiming to continue providing customer and community benefits. These programs have engaged over 480,000 customers and 20,000 businesses, resulting in nearly $900 million in collective annual energy savings and significant carbon emission reductions. The extension also seeks to maintain workforce continuity and local job creation through partnerships with trade allies and union labor.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI)
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP has issued a deadline alert for shareholders of Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI) who suffered losses, urging them to contact the firm regarding a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit stems from an indictment against Super Micro associates for allegedly diverting U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China, leading to a significant drop in the company's stock price. Investors who purchased SMCI securities between April 30, 2024, and March 19, 2026, have until May 26, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: PAYPAL HOLDINGS, INC. (PYPL) SHAREHOLDER ALERT Bernstein Liebhard LLP Reminds PayPal Holdings, Inc. Investors of Upcoming Deadline
feed.summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP is alerting PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) investors about an upcoming deadline in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. Investors who purchased PayPal shares between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026, and incurred losses, are encouraged to contact the firm to discuss their legal options, with a lead plaintiff deadline of April 20, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal and its senior officers made misrepresentations regarding the company's 2027 financial targets and the growth of its core branded checkout segment.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum Shares Surge 22.5% Amid Oil Price Spike
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) saw its shares surge 22.5% due to a more than 50% spike in oil prices in March, driven by the Iran war. The company has also focused on capital expenditure efficiency, reducing spending in the Permian Basin and aiming to repay a significant portion of its $20.4 billion debt acquired from Anadarko Petroleum in 2019. Analysts anticipate continued profitability and cash flow improvement if elevated oil prices persist, suggesting OXY might be undervalued.

feed.title: Gilead Acquisition Spree Continues With $3 Billion Tubulis Deal
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences is acquiring Tubulis GmbH for $3.15 billion upfront, with potential additional milestone payments, to bolster its oncology pipeline with TUB-040, an antibody-drug conjugate for ovarian cancer. This acquisition follows other recent deals, including Ouro Medicines and Arcellx, highlighting Gilead's strategy to expand its portfolio in oncology and autoimmune diseases. Despite the strategic acquisitions, Gilead's stock (GILD) showed short-term weakness but maintained a positive long-term outlook.

feed.title: Kraft Heinz recalls nearly 370,000 pounds of turkey bacon over listeria concern
feed.summary: Kraft Heinz is recalling nearly 370,000 pounds of fully cooked turkey bacon products, including several Oscar Mayer varieties, due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. The affected products were produced between April 24 and June 11, 2025, and distributed nationwide. Although there have been no reported illnesses, consumers are advised to discard or return the recalled items due to the serious health risks associated with Listeria.

feed.title: Barclays Maintains HST Rating, Raises Price Target to $20.00 | HST
feed.summary: Barclays analyst Richard Hightower has reaffirmed an "Equal-Weight" rating for Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) and increased the price target from $19.00 to $20.00, reflecting a 5.26% hike. This adjustment indicates a moderately positive outlook for the stock. Host Hotels & Resorts Inc. owns 78 primarily urban and resort hotel properties, mainly in the United States, operating under brands like Marriott and Starwood.

feed.title: Regions Financial (RF) Analyst Rating Update: Target Price Lowered | RF Stock News
feed.summary: JP Morgan has maintained its 'Neutral' rating on Regions Financial (RF) but lowered its target price to $29.00 from $31.50, a 7.94% decrease. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook, following similar target price reductions from other firms like Morgan Stanley, Piper Sandler, and Truist Securities in March 2026. Despite the lowered target, the average analyst forecast still suggests a 14.08% upside from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a potential downside based on its fair value calculation.

feed.title: JP Morgan Lowers Price Target for PNC While Maintaining Overweight Rating | PNC Stock News
feed.summary: JP Morgan has maintained an "Overweight" rating on PNC Financial Services Group (PNC) but has lowered its price target to $237.50 from $251.00, a 5.38% reduction. This adjustment comes amidst a pattern of lowered expectations from various analysts, though the consensus recommendation for PNC remains "Outperform." Despite these adjustments, the average target price of $243.62 from 22 analysts implies a 14.33% upside from the current price.

feed.title: Merck Begins Tender Offer to Acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
feed.summary: Merck has officially commenced a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Terns Pharmaceuticals, Inc. for $53.00 per share, following a definitive agreement announced on March 25, 2026. The tender offer is set to expire on May 4, 2026, and is subject to conditions including the tender of over 50% of Terns' shares and regulatory approvals. This acquisition is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, making Terns a wholly owned subsidiary of Merck.

feed.title: Deere & Co agrees to pay $99 million to settle 'right to repair' lawsuit
feed.summary: Deere & Co. has agreed to a $99 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit accusing the company of monopolizing repair services for its farm equipment. The lawsuit, filed in 2022, alleged that Deere withheld repair software and conspired with dealers to force farmers to use their services. The agreement, which still requires court approval, includes a settlement fund for affected customers and additional injunctive relief aimed at improving the availability of repair resources.

feed.title: Deere & Co agrees to pay $99 million to settle 'right to repair' lawsuit
feed.summary: Deere & Co. has agreed to a $99 million settlement in a class action lawsuit accusing the company of monopolizing repair services and violating "right to repair" principles. The lawsuit alleged Deere withheld repair software and conspired with dealers to force farmers into using authorized services at inflated prices. While denying wrongdoing, Deere stated the settlement aims to move forward and support customer access to repair resources, though it still faces separate litigation from the Federal Trade Commission on similar grounds.

feed.title: Raymond James Cuts MSCI Price Target to $700 From $710, Maintains Strong Buy Rating
feed.summary: Raymond James has adjusted its price target for MSCI Inc. (MSCI) from $710 to $700, while reiterating a "Strong Buy" rating on the stock. This update comes despite a slight reduction in the price target, indicating continued confidence in the company's performance. The article also notes other recent analyst adjustments for MSCI, including target changes from Goldman Sachs and Wells Fargo.

feed.title: Pebblebrook Hotel (PEB): Barclays Lowers Price Target, Maintains Underweight Rating | PEB Stock News
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for Pebblebrook Hotel (PEB) from $10.00 to $9.00, while maintaining an Underweight rating. This decision comes amidst varied analyst opinions, with some firms raising price targets and others keeping hold or neutral ratings. The average analyst target price for PEB is $12.65, implying a slight downside, though GuruFocus estimates a one-year fair value (GF Value) of $15.71, suggesting potential upside.

feed.title: JP Morgan Lowers Price Target for Wells Fargo (WFC) to $91 | WFC Stock News
feed.summary: JP Morgan has maintained a Neutral rating for Wells Fargo (WFC) but lowered its price target to $91 from $99.50, an 8.54% decrease. This adjustment follows several other banks also lowering their price targets for WFC in recent weeks. Despite these reductions, the average target price from 23 analysts is $98.93, suggesting a 21.95% upside from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a 7.81% downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Texas Gulf Bank hires former Prosperity Bank exec as president just months after naming new CEO
feed.summary: Texas Gulf Bank has appointed Chase Zalman, formerly of Prosperity Bank, as its new president. This hire comes less than two months after the bank named a new CEO, who also came from Prosperity Bank. The article highlights a trend of leadership changes at Texas Gulf Bank originating from Houston-based Prosperity Bank.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Expands Store-Based Order Fulfillment to 1,000+ Stores - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty has significantly expanded its store-based order fulfillment network, now utilizing over 1,000 U.S. stores to process online orders, doubling its capacity from the previous fiscal year. This expansion, aided by a new order management system, aligns with strategies used by other major retailers like Walmart and Target. The company also announced plans to build a new distribution center in the Northwest to further enhance its network and speed, alongside reporting increased net sales for its fourth quarter and full fiscal year.

feed.title: Praxis Adds To Neuroscience Momentum With Epilepsy Trial Success
feed.summary: Praxis Precision Medicines announced successful trial results for an epilepsy treatment, contributing to their neuroscience pipeline. This news follows a previous report anticipating a blockbuster future for the company's precision neurology drugs. The article highlights Praxis's continued progress in developing treatments for neurological disorders.

feed.title: Messenger RNA (mRNA) Market Projected to Reach $20.99 Billion by 2035 - Moderna, Pfizer, and Others Propel mRNA Expansion into New Therapeutic Areas
feed.summary: The global Messenger RNA (mRNA) market is set to expand significantly, reaching an estimated $20.99 billion by 2035, driven by its application in treating and preventing diseases, particularly in vaccines and therapeutics. This growth is bolstered by strong clinical validation, rapid development timelines, substantial investments, and advancements in delivery technologies. North America is a leading region due to its regulatory framework and manufacturing capabilities, with key players like Moderna and Pfizer continuing to innovate in this space.

feed.title: Starbucks Quietly Launches New Spring Drinks—Including a Caffeinated “Energy Refresher” Line
feed.summary: Starbucks has launched nine new drinks for a late spring release, featuring a new "Energy Refresher" line with approximately 75 mg of extra caffeine and a "Mango Cream Cold Foam." The new offerings include various mango-strawberry refresher options, new matcha and chai drinks with mango cream, and an Iced Ube Coconut Cream Shaken Espresso, following the success of a previous ube macchiato.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Gartner, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has announced a class action lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) and certain officers, alleging federal securities law violations. The lawsuit claims that Gartner made false and misleading statements and failed to disclose material adverse facts regarding its business, operations, and prospects during the Class Period from February 4, 2025, to February 2, 2026. Investors who purchased Gartner securities during this period are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of May 18, 2026.

feed.title: Hologic goes private in $79 per share buyout by Blackstone, TPG
feed.summary: Hologic Inc. has transitioned to private ownership in a $79 per share buyout by funds managed by Blackstone and TPG, with minority investments from Abu Dhabi Investment Authority and GIC. Joe Almeida has been appointed CEO, replacing Stephen MacMillan. Stockholders received $76 per share in cash plus a non-tradable contingent value right of up to $3 per share, dependent on future Breast Health business revenue goals.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) Shareholders
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP has issued a deadline alert for PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) shareholders who suffered losses, urging them to contact the firm regarding a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit stems from a stock price drop after PayPal announced a leadership change and admitted to underperformance in "branded checkout" during its Q4 2025 earnings report. Investors who purchased PayPal stock between February 8, 2024, and February 2, 2026, have until April 20, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: New Era Energy & Digital, Inc. (NUAI)
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP is reminding investors of New Era Energy & Digital, Inc. (NUAI) about an upcoming June 1, 2026 deadline to file a lead plaintiff motion in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that New Era Energy made false and misleading statements regarding its permitting progress and was involved in a fraudulent scheme to siphon revenue from oil and gas wells while dodging environmental cleanup responsibilities, leading to significant stock price drops. Investors who suffered losses between November 6, 2024, and December 29, 2025, are urged to contact the firm.

feed.title: Caesars Casino Bonus Code ROTOCASLAUNCH in April: Score $1k & 2.5K Rewards Credits
feed.summary: The article details the Caesars Casino bonus code ROTOCASLAUNCH for April 2026, offering new players a $10 no-deposit bonus, a 100% deposit match up to $1,000, and 2,500 Caesars Rewards Credits. It outlines how to claim the bonus, the associated wagering requirements, and explains the integrated Caesars Rewards program. The promotion is available in Michigan, New Jersey, West Virginia, and Pennsylvania, providing a comprehensive welcome package for online casino players.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Pinterest, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Pinterest, Inc. (NYSE: PINS) and certain officers, alleging federal securities law violations. The lawsuit encourages investors who purchased Pinterest securities between February 7, 2025, and February 12, 2026, to join to recover damages. It claims Pinterest made false or misleading statements regarding reduced advertising revenues, its ability to manage U.S. tariffs' impact, and an imminent restructuring.

feed.title: Analysts Are Bullish on These Financial Stocks: National Bank of Canada (NTIOF), Apollo Global Management (APO)
feed.summary: This article highlights bullish analyst sentiments for several financial stocks. BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating for National Bank of Canada (NTIOF) with a C$195 price target, while Bank of America Securities analysts set Buy ratings for Apollo Global Management (APO) at $139 and Northern (NTRS) with an undisclosed price target which has an analyst consensus of Hold but an 8.4% upside. The article provides details on the analysts' ratings, price targets, and their professional success rates.

feed.title: PG (NYSE: PG) health care CEO Paul Gama details stock, options and RSUs
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble Co.'s CEO of Health Care, Paul Gama, has filed an initial ownership report detailing his equity interests in the company. The report outlines his direct holdings of over 35,000 common shares, along with various stock options and Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) that will settle in P&G common stock or cash. Additionally, the filing notes indirect holdings through a retirement plan trustee, including common and Series A preferred stock.

feed.title: Wall Street Bullish on The Boeing Company (BA) Despite Recent Price Target Cuts
feed.summary: Despite recent price target cuts from analysts like Citigroup's John Godyn, Wall Street remains bullish on The Boeing Company (BA), maintaining a "Strong Buy" rating with a potential share price upside of 32%. Jefferies also reiterated a "Buy" rating, and Wells Fargo initiated coverage with an "Overweight" rating, citing anticipated sharp recovery in free cash flow and improving production rates. Boeing also recently secured a seven-year framework agreement to triple PAC-3 seeker production for air and missile defense.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Initiates Coverage on General Dynamics Corporation (GD) with Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo initiated coverage on General Dynamics Corporation (GD) with an Overweight rating and a $400 price target, citing an improving operating environment for shipbuilding, strong demand for military vehicles, and a business jet product lineup refresh. This positive outlook follows a recent $15.38 billion Navy contract modification for Columbia-class submarines. Although Citigroup recently trimmed its price target to $380, the consensus target for GD remains at $399.73, indicating a 15% upside.

feed.title: MetLife Investment Management Hires New Head of Real Estate, Agriculture From Blackstone
feed.summary: MetLife Investment Management has appointed Andrea Drasites as its new global head of real estate and agricultural finance, effective October 1. She will oversee $108.9 billion in assets and succeeds Robert Merck. Drasites joins MIM from Blackstone’s real estate group, where she served as a senior managing director for 13 years.

feed.title: Q1 Preview: Citigroup Remains Neutral on Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT)
feed.summary: Citigroup maintained a Neutral rating on Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT) and raised its price target slightly to $675 from $673 in anticipation of its first-quarter earnings. Meanwhile, Wells Fargo initiated coverage with an Equal-Weight rating and a $650 price target, citing expectations of slower growth, weaker free cash flow, and margin pressure from fixed-price programs. Wall Street currently holds a "Hold" rating on LMT with an average one-year price target of $672.50.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin Is Up 26% in 2026. Is LMT Still Worth Buying?
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin (LMT) has seen a 26% increase in its stock price year-to-date in 2026, driven by a record $194 billion backlog and new framework agreements for missile production. While the stock trades at a discount to its sector median based on NTM P/E, near-term margin dilution due to production ramps presents a credible bear argument. The article concludes that LMT offers a reasonable return for long-term investors if execution is clean, but much of the near-term upside has already been captured, with future growth dependent on successful missile production and favorable defense budgets.
